speaker: interviewer
text: --21, 1996. This is an interview with Leib-- L-E-I-B-- Dimerman-- D-I-M-E-R-M-A-N. Interview is being conducted in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada. You had mentioned that you were working for Mr. [? Steinman ?] and living in the Perth Apartments around the middle of the 1960s What did you do, at that time?

speaker: interviewee
text: A salesman.

speaker: interviewer
text: Selling what?

speaker: interviewee
text: Furniture store. The reason what I changed this place when I-- which I work like a welder, and I sweat, there's no opportunity to-- to be a welder. To go into competition to start independent welding is connected with money. And money, I didn't have it. No chances to have money. Then I decided to change my profession. And a salesman, I can have opportunity to start with nothing, with-- with less with nothing, and to be a sale-- a businessman for myself. And this is the reason what the-- this-- the mind-- what forced me to create and to start to work for Steinman.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mr. [? Wolinsky, ?] we was friends with him-- Mrs. and Mr. [? Wolinsky-- ?] remember? And they recommended me. And [? Wolinsky ?] used to know Mr. [? Steinman. ?] Through [? Wolinsky, ?] we get the job. And how long did you work for Mr. Steinman?

speaker: interviewee
text: Two year-- two years.

speaker: interviewer
text: And, after the two years?

speaker: interviewee
text: After [? Steinman, ?] I start [? on ?] [? myself. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: Doing what?

speaker: interviewee
text: Secondhand store. This is my start.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what have you done since then?

speaker: interviewee
text: Then I would say [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Canada's a country which, [INAUDIBLE] the countries, hard work. And properly spending. To look what [? we ?] spending your dollar. And this gave me a-- a full opportunity to go slowly, step by step. Be here, and be here. And then open up a big furniture store-- brand-new, and I call myself Sanyo Music Center.

speaker: interviewer
text: 'Sunny'?

speaker: interviewee
text: 'Sanyo'--

speaker: interviewer
text: Sanyo.

speaker: interviewee
text: --Music Center. Which remains the most beautiful store I finished up. And after the store is finished, I put in furniture, with a beautiful sign outside. Sanyo Company put up a sign for me. And when I've gone to take insurance, they told me they can't give me the insurance. The block didn't have a sprinkle system-- [? LaSalle ?] Apartment-- they didn't have a sprinkler system. And next door from me was a restaurant. This is the reason they didn't give me insurance. And the guy from the restaurant some way received the insurance, and he make a fire. And I burned out. Everything is gone. Just debts left behind. And I start again from the beginning.

speaker: interviewer
text: And this was 1960--

speaker: interviewee
text: No. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] It was already '70-something.

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewee
text: Then I start again, from the beginning. Again, a secondhand store. And to straighten myself. And in present time, I have a furniture store, and I am a professional appraiser.

speaker: interviewer
text: Appraiser.

speaker: interviewee
text: And I have a loan company-- a [? Patent ?] [? Broking ?] Loan Company.

speaker: interviewer
text: And did you have more children, after Joey?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, was born here, in Canada-- was born Sheldon, and next is Ian. The last [? was ?] Ian. What is present time living with us.

speaker: interviewer
text: When was Sheldon born?

speaker: interviewee
text: 19-- [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] 1976?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE] .

speaker: interviewer
text: And Ian is-- was born in 1970? [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] [BACKROUND NOISE] So what kind of activities are you involved in, outside of work, at the moment?

speaker: interviewee
text: Outside activities, I'm healing people. I'm-- This is what I discovered, being in jail. When I was in Siberia, I discovered I have power-- electrical hand-- by healing people. Which I don't credit myself. This is just a gift from God. And--

speaker: interviewer
text: And what do you do with that skill?

speaker: interviewee
text: I am just helping people, by opportunity, when I have time. And I'm not out advertising myself. I don't have the time. It's only friends and people what was recommended already to me, I'm helping them.

speaker: interviewer
text: How do you help them? How do you help them with your healing?

speaker: interviewee
text: Just by holding my hand and touching their body.

speaker: interviewer
text: And, aside from your healing skill, what other things are you involved with?

speaker: interviewee
text: I like to read, which I don't have the time. Lots of time, I fall asleep with the book in my hand. And, when I was a little bit younger, I used to like skis-- skis-- skating, over here, beside my house, is a skate. Swimming-- fishing. When I have the time-- which is very hard, to get the time.

speaker: interviewer
text: What message would you like to leave for people watching this film and for your family?

speaker: interviewee
text: The purpose for my interview right now, present time, I hope this will prevent future tragedy-- the tragedy what I saw with my eyes and my body felt. And not in future to divide kids from the parents. And this I hope-- the future generation, the kids, the children, the grandchildren will help to prevent the tragedy, in future. That's all. And this is my-- my wish.

speaker: interviewer
text: Could you introduce your wife, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Liuba Dimerman, present time, when I met the first time was Liuba [? Katz. ?]

speaker: interviewee
text: [LAUGH]

speaker: interviewee
text: And I met my wife in Russia, before I marry here.

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE]

speaker: interviewee
text: And the present time she's Liuba Dimerman-- my wife.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where are your children located, right now?

speaker: interviewee
text: My-- my older son is a doctor in Toronto. He practices-- he practice alone-- acupuncture and chiropractor. And my middle guy is Sheldon Dimerman-- he is-- he is right now in Israel, which is self-- self-independent-- is employed. He have a photo shop in [? core suburb, ?] and married. He had a lovely wife and two beautiful grandchildren.

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE] granddaughter.

speaker: interviewee
text: I said lovely, beautiful wife-- nice.

speaker: interviewee
text: No, Joey [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewee
text: Oh, yeah, Joey, my son. Yeah, I have a grandchild, too, I forgot. Yeah.

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE] .

speaker: interviewer
text: In Toronto? And your youngest son?

speaker: interviewee
text: My youngest son just finished university in Toronto. Master which-- in Business Administration. And he arrived home right now and is helping me in my business. And in future, I don't know what is going to do with his plans.

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, he's-- he'll be successful, for sure.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is his plans, I don't know.

speaker: interviewer
text: And is there anything you would like to say to your husband, at this point?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, I think he's deserving retirement. And he worked hard enough. He went through lots in his life. And it's time to retire and enjoy whatever is given by God.

speaker: interviewer
text: Could you tell us about this picture, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken in 1939, before the-- the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who is it?

speaker: interviewee
text: My father-- Yontel Dimerman.

speaker: interviewer
text: And it was taken where?

speaker: interviewee
text: Taken in Kostopol, in the same town where we used to live. And this is my mother, Chana Dimerman. The picture was taken in Kostopol, in 1939. Which the same city, the same town we was living. And she was born in the same city, Kostopol.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, and who is in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: [BACKROUND NOISE] In the left side, in corner-- yeah, over there-- you can see it. This, I am, in 1937, behind the school-- public school. And I am dressed in the school uniform.

speaker: interviewer
text: [BACKROUND NOISE] And this is a class picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, from school-- yeah. I think it was in grade-- in this time, in grade four.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Could you tell us about this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: This when I was an officer in the army-- a young [BACKROUND NOISE] lieutenant. And behind is the house I used to sleep over there. We made it-- we called a surviving home. It was cold outside. And behind is the tank. I was operator in the Joseph Stalin. Big tank. Huge tank. I was behind the-- the tanks over there, far away, you can see it-- the tanks. And this is the little house. We used to sleep three people, four people, in this.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: That was in 1940-- 1944-- end of '44.

speaker: interviewer
text: Taken where?

speaker: interviewee
text: Around Czechoslovakia. This is my graduation day, when I was a cadet. And I was graduated. Which the other picture what you saw, this was already I was in the full uniform. And this is the uniform [BACKROUND NOISE] from the cadets in the academy.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year?

speaker: interviewee
text: 19-- end of 1944. Not end, exactly. I want to say the month was nine, 1944.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: [? Grosne. ?] In the academy. Yeah, this picture is 1945, in July. Already was after the war, I think. This is-- I think the-- I make a picture in the day what they let me free.

speaker: interviewer
text: They discharged you?

speaker: interviewee
text: They let me go-- discharged from the army.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was taken in Schwarzwald, in Germany. This when I was already a performer, after the war. And this was 1946. And this was in the city Leningrad, was everything the ruins, behind. The city was very badly destroyed. And I'm in front of the ruin-- ruins, over there, from the blocks behind-- Leningrad. 1946, fall time. This, I'm [INAUDIBLE] the right side, the smaller little guy, I am. And the taller guy's my friend. And be used to perform charity concerts in Yiddish, like [INAUDIBLE] and [INAUDIBLE]. I used to play [INAUDIBLE]. And he was a performer, too.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was 1947.

speaker: interviewer
text: In-- where?

speaker: interviewee
text: 1947. The photo was taken in Rowne. [BACKROUND NOISE] Now this is my mother, Chana Dimerman, and Moshe, my younger brother, in the right side-- the small, little guy. And from the left side is Meyer, is born in 1931 and was killed [INAUDIBLE]. He was buried like a hero. He covered up with his body an Arab tank which his friend put a molotov bomb. And they stopped the tank to come into the Kibbutz Kinneret. And he's known in Israel. And over there, they have a gun under his name-- Meyer. The gun Meyer. And they have the forest in Kinneret. Beside the Kibbutz Kinneret is named Meyer. The forest name is under his name, Meyer.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken in 1940-- '46. This is-- on the right side is my brother, Meyer, like I already told you previously. He was [INAUDIBLE]. He was killed in Israel, like a hero. If-- he tried to protect the-- not to come in the tank, the Arab tank, to the Kibbutz Kinneret, which only the children was over there-- kids. And it was supervising the small kids. Very talented guy. He was talented in poetry-- writing. Killed young.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the other person?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is Meyer.

speaker: interviewee
text: Moshe.

speaker: interviewee
text: Moshe. He was in present time in Israel. Very qualified, high in the army. And present time is teaching in the military academy.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Israel, 1946. This picture was in 1947 It was-- came with a performance in Moscow. Was taken in Moscow, in 1947. I was in this time a performer-- magician. This is a magic trick. I put up a performance in Israel, with a stick disappearing.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year?

speaker: interviewee
text: In 19-- 1961? This is given to me, a paper, from the Russian authorities, from KGB, by changing my name-- which, I mentioned before, I was-- I changed my name. I was [? Sempatia ?] [? Felix-- ?] nationality, French-- French. And I change back to my original name, Leon Dimerman.

speaker: interviewer
text: This is the command [INAUDIBLE]. Leon Dimerman, previous [? Felix ?] [? Felixovich ?] and [? Sempatia. ?] What year was this issued to you?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is in 1945.

speaker: interviewee
text: '57, [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewee
text: Pardon me?

speaker: interviewee
text: '57. That document is '57-- '57.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. This is my sister, Edna. And was taken, the picture in Toronto, in 1986. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] She's present time living in Winnipeg. In the right hand is my brother Moshe, like you saw previous-- the small, little guy in the picture-- my youngest brother. In the middle I am, Leib Dimerman. And to the right, this is my wife-- my brother's wife, [? Lucia. ?] And behind is Golda Dimerman. This is Golda Schweig, present time-- [BACKROUND NOISE] marriage name. This is my sister Golda, and she's in present time-- she's living in Israel.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Picture taken I think before I left. 19-- 1960--

speaker: interviewee
text: No, no, no.

speaker: interviewee
text: What, no?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE]

speaker: interviewee
text: No. Leon, it's already later.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my son Ian. Is present time living with us, but he graduated a couple days ago, in Toronto University. Master in Business Administration. My daughter-in-law Sarah, present time in Toronto-- Sarah Dimerman. And to the left is my son Joey. He's practicing-- he's a doctor in Toronto. And this is my grandchild, in the middle. This is my son, the middle son, Sheldon Dimerman, which is living present time in Israel. And this is his wife, [? Asnot ?] Dimerman. And this picture is taken in day from the wedding. We celebrated their wedding in Israel. In a beautiful park, we made the wedding. These are my two grandchildren-- two little boys. From the right is the older son-- grandsons-- Sean. And this is my young little grandchild.
